# Copilot Instructions – ToolHitList (Power Apps Code App)

## Architecture

This is a **Power Apps code app** — a TypeScript SPA hosted and managed on the Power Platform.
Reference: [learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/developer/code-apps](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/developer/code-apps/)

```
app/                          # The code app (Vite + React 19 + Tailwind v4)
  src/
    components/               # Reusable UI components
    pages/                    # Route-level page components
    hooks/                    # Custom React hooks
    lib/                      # Utilities: cn() and helpers
    main.tsx                  # Entry point
    App.tsx                   # Root component
    index.css                 # Tailwind v4 entry (@import "tailwindcss")
  power.config.json           # Generated by npx power-apps init (committed, not secret)
  vite.config.ts              # Includes react(), tailwindcss(), powerApps() plugins
  package.json

docs/                         # Project brief, security design, ADRs
.github/workflows/ci.yml      # Build + lint on push/PR
```

**Key layers ([architecture](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/developer/code-apps/architecture)):**
- **`@microsoft/power-apps`** – Power Apps SDK; call `PowerApps.getConnector(...)` to access 1,500+ connectors from JavaScript
- **`@microsoft/power-apps-vite`** – Vite plugin integrating the SDK into the dev server and build
- **`power.config.json`** – generated metadata; created by `npx power-apps init`, updated by `pac code add-datasource`
- **Power Apps host** – manages Entra authentication, app loading, and DLP enforcement at runtime

## Build and Test

All commands run from `app/`:

```bash
# Authenticate (first time / new machine)
pac auth create
pac env select --environment <your-environment-id>

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Initialise code app — creates power.config.json (run once per environment)
npx power-apps init --displayName "ToolHitList" --environmentId <your-environment-id>

# Run locally (Vite plugin handles app + connection proxy)
npm run dev
# Open the URL labelled "Local Play" printed in the terminal
# Use the SAME browser profile as your Power Platform tenant

# Build & deploy to Power Apps (into the TaskList solution)
npm run build
npx power-apps push -s TaskList

# Lint
npm run lint

# Add a data connector (regenerates typed models into src/)
pac code add-datasource
```

> **Browser note (Dec 2025+):** Chrome/Edge block localhost from public origins by default.
> Grant local network access permission when prompted during `npm run dev`.

## Code Style

- **TypeScript** strict mode throughout — no `any`
- **Components**: functional components only; `cn()` from `lib/utils` for class merging
- **Tailwind v4** — utility classes only; `@import "tailwindcss"` in `index.css`; no custom CSS unless unavoidable
- **Data fetching**: Power Apps SDK connectors via `PowerApps.getConnector(...)` — no bare `fetch` in components
- **State**: `useState`/`useReducer` locally
- **File naming**: PascalCase for components, camelCase for hooks and utils

## Power Apps SDK Usage

```ts
import { PowerApps } from '@microsoft/power-apps';

// Get a typed connector (declared in power.config.json after pac code add-datasource)
const dataverse = PowerApps.getConnector('shared_commondataserviceforapps');

// Call an action
const result = await dataverse.listRecords({ entityName: 'jsdev_items' });
```

- Add a data source: `pac code add-datasource` — regenerates typed models in `src/`
- All connector calls go through the SDK — never call Dataverse REST or connector APIs directly
- Connection references are environment-agnostic; actual connections resolved at runtime by the host

## Project Conventions

- Publisher prefix: **`jsdev`** — all Dataverse tables/columns use this (e.g., `jsdev_toolhitlist`)
- `power.config.json` is committed but never contains secrets; environment binding is done via `pac env select`
- The `app/` directory is the deployable unit — do not put app source in the repo root
- Code apps **do not support** the solution packager or Power Platform Git integration
- ALM is via `npx power-apps push -s TaskList` and Power Platform Pipelines

## ALM – Environment Promotion

| Environment | Purpose | Branch |
|-------------|---------|--------|
| Dev | Active development | `feature/*` |
| Test/UAT | Validation | `main` |
| Production | Live | tagged release |

- Deploy: `npm run build && npx power-apps push -s TaskList` targeting `pac auth` active environment
- Solution unique name: **`TaskList`** | Display name: **Task List** | Publisher prefix: **`jsdev`**
- Promote: use [Power Platform Pipelines](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/alm/pipelines)
- Post-deploy: verify connection reference assignments, confirm env variable values, smoke-test key pages

## Prerequisites (one-time per machine)

- Node.js LTS
- PAC CLI (installed via MSI — verify with `pac`)
- Power Apps environment feature enabled: Power Platform admin centre → Settings → Features → Enable code apps
- End-users need **Power Apps Premium** licence

## Integration Points

- **Dataverse** – via Power Apps SDK connector (`shared_commondataserviceforapps`)
- **Power Automate** – via SDK flow connector
- **Copilot Studio** – via `pac code add-datasource` ([docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-apps/developer/code-apps/how-to/connect-to-copilot-studio))
- **Azure SQL / SharePoint** – additional connectors via `pac code add-datasource`

## Security

- Authentication is entirely handled by the Power Apps host (Entra ID) — no auth code in the app
- Never call connectors or Dataverse directly — DLP policies are only enforced through the SDK/host
- Do not commit `.env` files or credentials; use PAC CLI auth profiles per-machine
- Column/row-level security defined in Dataverse; document in [docs/security.md](../docs/security.md)
